meaning: adj.短暂的,瞬时的n.瞬时现象
examples: The studies discussed so far exposed people to noise for only short periods and only transient effects were studied.
For example, studies show that even brief periods of odorant stimulation produce transient reductions in receptors in the olfactory epithelium, a process termed "receptor fatigue".
It is argued that the influence of transient investment on public companies can be adverse.
We are, rather, seeing the transient effects of the opening up of global trade.
Moreover, at BGI most faculty are transient and paid for what they are, temporary, part-time teachers.
